title: rubia borealis
intro: Rubia borealis, also known as the Arctic madder or Alaskan madder, is a perennial plant species in the madder family, Rubiaceae. The plant's root contains rubiadin and other anthraquinone compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The root of Rubia borealis has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including diarrhea, menstrual disorders, and skin conditions, due to its astringent, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The herbal preparations of Rubia borealis include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to take advantage of its medicinal properties and health benefits.
